description: baseline: HEAD af421db + setup, 5 sweeps via REPS=5 ./autoresearch.sh

Cut grounder lookup+alloc overhead: flat-vector MapVariablePosition (single scan, no double lookup), JoinHashTable.clear() instead of realloc in clean_up, reused join key buffer. Behavior-preserving (reference + 62/62 checks). KEEP vs run1 (3.50x, +17.5%) and vs pooled clean baseline (2.71x, +15.2%).

reached_facts+newfacts as reused members (retain buckets across ground()) + hoist join inner-loop head-position lookup. Behavior-preserving (gate passed) but no improvement over run 2 (median 72.4 vs 67.0, DISCARD) and +6MB peak. Once the run-2 wins are in, per-state set/vector allocs and the join hoist are not a dominant cost.

Achievers by-value sink ctor + std::move at join/product sites (kill double vector<int> alloc) + is_cheapest_path lazy_emplace with in-place cost mutation (single hash instead of find+erase+insert). Behavior-preserving (62/62 checks, gate passed). Contemporaneous A/B vs freshly-measured run-2 HEAD: candidate 63.162 vs run-2 63.227 -> 0.1%, confidence 0.02x = NO real gain. The apparent ~6% vs stored run-2 best (67.0) was stored-baseline drift, not a real win. Lesson: grounder micro-allocation cuts don't help past run-2 (glibc tcache); compare candidates against a FRESH contemporaneous HEAD measurement, never stale stored samples.

Successor-gen batch: (1) hoist the std::sort out of SparseStatePacker::pack's relation loop (R sorts -> 1; final sorted vector identical) + drop a dead unused 'packed_relation' alloc; (2) apply_lifted_action_effects: replace an O(n) std::find over the unordered_set with insert().second (O(1)). Both behavior-preserving (62/62 checks, gate passed) and simpler. Contemporaneous A/B (batch vs freshly-built run-2 HEAD, pooled 10v10): batch 62.83 vs HEAD 65.23 = consistent ~3.7% win, but confidence 0.65x and the win is BELOW the baseline noise floor (~4.85% CV), so unconfirmable on this machine (decide.py min-rel gate needs win > baseline CV). Real but sub-floor. Diff saved to autoresearch-data/pending-succ-gen-wins.patch for future bundling.

Cut redundant successor-generation work (full_reducer = ~61% of suite). (1) filter_static: track applied static (in)equality preconds with a per-instantiate bitset, skip re-filtering after later joins (no-op once columns present); enabled for generic/full_reducer/ordered linear-join generators, Yannakakis kept original (projects columns, breaks invariant). (2) hoist std::sort out of SparseStatePacker::pack's relation loop + drop dead alloc. (3) apply_lifted_action_effects O(n) std::find over unordered_set -> insert().second O(1). Behavior-preserving (gate + 62/62 checks). KEEP: contemporaneous A/B vs freshly-built run-2 HEAD = 59.53 vs 66.02, +9.83%, confidence 2.58x. Bundles the run-5 saved patch + the filter_static dedup (the latter was the decisive addition).

Precompute get_indices_and_constants per fluent atom into PrecompiledActionData (the code's own TODO; removes per-state recompute in parse_precond_into_join_program) + pack() reserve total tuples instead of num_predicates. Behavior-preserving (62/62). Contemporaneous A/B vs fresh run-6 HEAD: candidate 61.69 vs HEAD 59.20 = NO improvement (slightly worse, within noise). Confirms again: state-independent recompute + reserve micro-opts don't move the metric once the algorithmic redundancies are gone. The recompute was tiny and not the bottleneck.

Join-path bundle: (1) hash_join's std::unordered_map and hash_semi_join's std::unordered_set -> phmap (SwissTable), faster, behavior-preserving (output order depends on t1/t2 iteration, not map layout); (2) hash_join build map stores INDICES into t1.tuples instead of copying whole tuples (valid: t1.tuples unchanged until final move). Behavior-preserving (62/62 + gate). Contemporaneous A/B vs run-6 HEAD, pooled 15v15 over 3 rounds: bundle median 58.38 vs HEAD 61.11 = consistent ~4.5% (mean 3.7%) AND deterministic peak_mem 139 vs 149 MB (-7%), but confidence only 0.66x -- the ~4.5% time win is below this machine's effective noise floor (~6.6%, inflated by persistent slow-sweep outliers), so unconfirmable like runs 4/5/7. Real + clean + memory win, but sub-floor. Patch saved: autoresearch-data/pending-phmap-join-wins.patch (git apply to re-add; bundle with a 3rd join-path win to cross the gate).

Join-path 3-part bundle = run-8 phmap+index patch PLUS a 3rd win: in-place stable compaction in hash_semi_join (move surviving tuples down instead of copying into a fresh new_tuples vector; behavior-identical, drops one vector alloc + N tuple copies per semi-join). Behavior-preserving (gate + 62/62 checks). Contemporaneous A/B vs fresh run-6 HEAD (REPS=5 each): candidate median 54.74 vs HEAD 57.47 = +4.75%, peak_mem deterministic 139 vs 149 MB (-6.7%). decide.py DISCARD: separable (confidence 5.74x) but 4.75% is below this round's noise floor 6.90% (HEAD CV, inflated by 2 slow sweeps 66/63; machine's true CV 5-9%). The 5.74x is a small-sample MAD artifact; run 8's 15v15 pool already showed the pooled confidence is ~0.66x. The in-place semi-join compaction added only ~0.25% over run-8's ~4.5% => semi-join copies are NOT a bottleneck (glibc tcache + small tables, per run-4 lesson). The '3rd join-path win to cross 6%' strategy fails: the join path has no 6% in it. Real+clean+memory win but sub-floor like runs 4/5/8. Superseding bundle patch saved: autoresearch-data/pending-join-path-bundle.patch.

SBO for the Datalog grounder's per-fact heap vectors: Arguments (vector<Term> -> boost::container::small_vector<Term,4>) and Achievers (vector<int> -> small_vector<int,2>). Every Fact stored one tiny heap buffer per member, and millions of facts are produced per ground() call; inlining them removes those allocations AND the pointer indirection on every fact hash/compare/copy in the fixpoint (the cache-locality axis run 4 never tested -- run 4 only cut alloc COUNT, which tcache makes free). Behavior-preserving (pure storage change: same elements/order/hash/==; 62/62 checks + reference gate pass). Profile motivation: grounder = 63% of suite (WeightedGrounder::join 11% + product/ground + malloc ~16%). Contemporaneous A/B (REPS=5 each): candidate median 45.96 vs fresh HEAD 58.29 = +21.15%, confidence 18.16x, floor 6.46%. KEEP. peak_mem unchanged at 149 MB (bigger Fact offset by removed heap fragmentation). One-line fix needed in join.h (qualify std::find/std::distance, previously ADL on std iterators). NOTE: introduces a header-only system-boost include (no CMake/link change needed); follow-up could vendor a minimal small_vector to match the project's vendored-phmap, no-system-deps style.

Re-applied the saved join-path bundle (phmap in hash_join/hash_semi_join + index-store in hash_join build map + in-place semi-join compaction) on top of run 10, betting that the grounder's 21% speedup made the bundle's ~2.7s absolute saving a bigger relative fraction (~6%). Behavior-preserving (62/62 + gate). Contemporaneous A/B, pooled 15v15 over 3 rounds: candidate median 43.85 vs run-10 HEAD 46.78 = +6.05% time AND deterministic peak_mem 132 vs 149 MB (-11.4%, compounding grounder-SBO + join-phmap). decide.py: RERUN->DISCARD -- improvement clears the floor (~6% vs 6.25%) but confidence stalls at 1.56x (<2.0): slow-sweep outliers in both groups inflate the robust sigma and the distributions overlap. SAME wall as runs 8/9: the join-path win (~4.5-6%, conf 0.66-1.73x across runs 8/9/11) is intrinsically unconfirmable on this box regardless of baseline -- it's a measurement limitation, not a fake win (median always positive; phmap+less-memory is machine-independently beneficial). Discarded per the 'confirm at most ~2x then discard' protocol. Patch kept: autoresearch-data/pending-join-path-bundle.patch. RECOMMENDATION for a human who values the -11% memory win: apply it; it's clean and behavior-identical.

SBO on the successor-gen path (run-10 lens applied to the state/join side): GroundAtom (std::vector<int> -> boost small_vector<int,4>) and Table::tuple_t (same type), so relation tuples and all join intermediates (hash_join/hash_semi_join/project/cartesian new_tuples, filter_static) store inline. Targets the blind/full_reducer profile (hash_semi_join 18% + ~21% malloc, untouched by run 10). Behavior-preserving: TupleHash/utils::feed are content-based, so set iteration order (=> packer index assignment => search trajectory) is identical; 62/62 + reference gate pass. Cascade: generalized TupleHash to a template, added utils::feed for small_vector, AtomicGoal/added_atoms/pack_tuple/order_tuple now use GroundAtom; LiftedOperatorId kept vector<int> (operator instantiation, not a tuple). Contemporaneous A/B pooled 10v10: candidate median 41.79 vs run-10 HEAD 47.68 = +12.35%, confidence 2.06x (borderline only due to within-run drift; effect 12-16% across all rounds). KEEP. TRADEOFF: peak_mem 149 -> 184 MB (+23%) -- small_vector inline storage is larger per tuple in the transient join tables + packer maps; well within the 8 GiB limit and time is the primary metric, but a real secondary regression. FOLLOW-UP (run 13): tune inline N (try 2/3) or decouple GroundAtom (small, persistent) from tuple_t (wider, transient) to recover memory while holding the speed. Patch: autoresearch-data/candidate-run12-groundatom-sbo.patch.
